methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $29.0B.

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Picogrid is a Seed-stage frontier-tech company operating in Defense & Security, founded by Zane Mountcastle. The company has raised $10M+ to date. Valuation analysis draws on a peer set of 10 comparable companies spanning public equities (2 firms) and private ventures (8 firms), including adjacent-stage Defense & Security firms such as Tenna Systems ($60M Seed), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Stark Defence ($500M Series A), and Scale AI ($29.0B Series A).

Two triangulation methods apply. Stage-progression analysis uses total raised ($10M) as a proxy base value and applies a 2.5x multiplier typical of Seed-to-next-stage advancement, yielding $25M. Peer-median analysis across the five most comparable Defense & Security companies at same or adjacent stage produces a median valuation of $300M (range: $60M to $29.0B). The wide upper bound reflects Scale AI's outlier position; the tight median better represents typical frontier-defense valuations at this stage.

These methods triangulate to a range of $21M (low) to $345M (high), with a median of $162M. The stage-progression method anchors the conservative end; peer median anchors the optimistic. Key uncertainties include whether Picogrid's technical defensibility and addressable market align with higher-end peers like Stark Defence or track closer to Seed-stage comparables like Tenna Systems, and execution risk inherent to early-stage hardware and defense applications.
